quality.rationale: 基于闭合FLRW度规的多步动力学关联与曲率空间体积积分

origin.question: 19.23 A closed $(\mathbf{k} = 1)$ Friedmann universe has Hubble constant $H_0$ and deceleration parameter $q_0$ . Assume that the universe has always been matter dominated.(c) What is the total proper volume now occupied by the matter which we see, looking out into the sky?
origin.solution: (c) The farthest coordinate radius that we see is, according to Equation (8),$$r_{0} = \sin (\eta_{0}) = (2 q_{0} - 1)^{\frac {1}{2}} / q_{0}$$where Equation (7) has been used. The volume out to this coordinate radius at the present epoch is (analogously to part (a))$$\begin{array}{l} V = 4 \pi \int_{0}^{\mathrm {r}_{0}} \frac {R_{0}^{3} \mathrm {r}^{2} \mathrm {d r}}{\left(1 - \mathrm {r}^{2}\right)_{-}^{\frac {1}{2}}} = 2 \pi R_{0}^{3} \left[ \sin^{- 1} \mathrm {r}_{0} - \mathrm {r}_{0} \left(1 - \mathrm {r}_{0}^{2}\right)^{\frac {1}{2}}\right] \\= \frac {2 \pi}{\mathrm {H}_{0}^{3} \left(2 \mathrm {q}_{0} - 1\right)^{\frac {3}{2}}} \left[ \cos^{- 1} \left(\frac {1 - \mathrm {q}_{0}}{\mathrm {q}_{0}}\right) - \frac {\left(1 - \mathrm {q}_{0}\right) \left(2 \mathrm {q}_{0} - 1\right)^{\frac {1}{2}}}{\mathrm {q}_{0}^{2}}\right]. \\\end{array}$$
